title: Save a massive $1,100 on this RTX 5080 gaming PC with a 9800X3D from HP, now just $2,499 liquid-cooled Omen 35L rig unlocks 4K gameplay with 32GB DDR5 and a 2TB SSD
summary: Save $1,100 on an HP Omen 45L gaming rig with a 9800X3D, RTX 5080, 32GB DDR5 RAM, and 2TB SSD storage for $2,499.99.
insight: Unlock 4K gaming capabilities at a significantly lower cost.

title: Pick up Hoto's ultra-useful 3D printing tool for just $29 save 40% on this 35-piece Cordless Rotary Tool to give your creations a finishing touch
summary: Hoto's cordless rotary tool is now available for $29, a 40% discount. This 35-piece tool is ideal for PC builders and hobbyists who need a finishing touch for their creations. It's a valuable addition to any DIY arsenal.
insight: Precision finishing for DIY projects at an affordable price point.

title: China s hollow-core fiber trial pushes 51.3 Tb/s over 128 miles without signal regeneration milestone targets AI-era networking bottlenecks
summary: China-based YOFC, China Telecom, and Dekoli achieved a record 51.3 Tb/s transmission over 206.5 km using hollow-core fiber without signal regeneration.
insight: Overcoming fiber limitations to boost data transmission capacity in AI-era networks.

title: PNY's Performance 32GB DDR5-5600 RAM becomes the cheapest 2x16GB kit DDR5 kit gets a $70 discount
summary: PNY's 32GB DDR5-5600 RAM kit receives a significant price drop to $70 less, making it a competitive option for those seeking affordable high-capacity memory.
insight: Affordable 32GB DDR5 RAM option emerges due to aggressive pricing.

title: Lenovo says the 'RAMageddon' is the new normal, outlines survival guide at ISC 2026 an exec said 'it will never be like it was last year'
summary: Lenovo claims 'RAMageddon' is the new normal in the memory market, stating that it will never return to last year's levels. This was announced at the International Supercomputing Conference 2026. The company is preparing users for a new reality.
insight: Rapid memory price increases become a permanent market condition reality.

title: AMD engineer 3D-prints Steam Machine-a-like with diagonal mobo mounting parts include a Mini ITX motherboard, RTX 5060, and a flex ATX PSU
summary: The Terk Box v1.1 is a 3D-printed PC case alternative to Valve's Steam Machine, featuring a Mini ITX motherboard, NVIDIA RTX 5060 GPU, and a flex ATX PSU, offering a unique build option for enthusiasts.
insight: DIY enthusiasts can create custom gaming PCs with minimal costs and space.

title: 400 domains used for illegal 2026 World Cup streams seized by US Justice Department operation is five times the scale of the previous crackdown
summary: The US Department of Justice seized nearly 400 domains for illegal 2026 World Cup streams, a significant crackdown five times larger than previous operations.
insight: Combating piracy helps protect intellectual property rights and fair revenue distribution.

title: China s Loongson launches homegrown 16-core server CPU built on LoongArch architecture 40W chip with DDR4 ECC and 32 PCIe lanes targets cheap SMB file, database, and web servers
summary: Loongson has launched the 3C3000, a 16-core server CPU based on LoongArch architecture, designed for cheap SMB servers.
insight: China's Loongson achieves competitive performance with a homegrown, affordable CPU design.

title: AI coding agents can be tricked into installing malware via 'clean' GitHub repositories Mozilla's 0din team shows how Claude Code can be exploited by its own helpfulness
summary: AI coding agents like Claude can be tricked into installing malware via 'clean' GitHub repositories by asking the bot to initialize a project, exploiting its helpfulness.
insight: Helpful AI tools can be manipulated into spreading malicious code.

title: PlayStation is removing over 500 movies from UK customers' accounts with no refunds Iconic films like Terminator 2, Apocalypse Now, and Mulholland Drive are getting deleted
summary: Sony is removing 551 movies from UK PlayStation users' accounts on September 1, 2026, due to expired licensing agreements with StudioCanal.
insight: Expired licensing agreements cause loss of iconic movies for UK users.

title: Waveshare ESP32-C6-Zero-B A tiny ESP32-C6 USB-C board with 5V-36V wide supply voltage
summary: The Waveshare ESP32-C6-Zero-B is a compact ESP32-C6 board with 5V-36V supply voltage support, featuring WiFi 6, Bluetooth 5.3, and 802.15.4 radio.
insight: Industrial-grade ESP32 board expands IoT applications with wide voltage support

title: Simplia CONNECT RW612 6-in-1 M.2 2230 module offers WiFi 6, Bluetooth 5.4, 802.15.4, LTE-M, NB-IoT, and GNSS connectivity
summary: Simplia CONNECT RW612 is a compact 6-in-1 wireless module offering dual-band WiFi 6, Bluetooth 5.4, and other connectivity options.
insight: First module to integrate multiple wireless standards into a small M.2 2230 module.

title: DEBIX T62P-01 Raspberry Pi-like industrial SBC features TI AM62P SoC, dual GbE with TSN, multi-domain UART
summary: The DEBIX T62P-01 is a compact industrial SBC built around the Texas Instruments Sitara AM62P SoC, designed for industrial IoT, robotics, and edge computing applications. It features two GbE ports, Wi-Fi 6, and Bluetooth 5.4 connectivity, along with advanced security features. The board supports up to 8GB of LPDDR4 memory and optional eMMC storage.
insight: Industrial IoT applications require secure, high-performance edge computing solutions.

title: DESLOC V150 Plus smart lock integrates a perovskite solar panel, supports 3D face recognition (Crowdfunding)
summary: The DESLOC V150 Plus smart lock features a perovskite solar panel, 3D face recognition, and fingerprint sensor, providing up to 8 months of backup operation without manual charging.
insight: Innovative solar-powered smart lock offers extended battery life autonomy.

title: Best of Sensors: Aizip wins startup of the year
summary: Aizip won Startup of the Year at Best of Sensors on May 6. Their AI at the edge technology provides distinct advantages, solidifying their position. The company's CTO, Weier Wan, shares insights on this innovative approach.
insight: AI at the edge enhances real-time decision-making and efficiency significantly

title: Future robotaxis may not need brake pedals under new US proposal
summary: A new US proposal may eliminate brake pedals in purpose-built robotaxis, focusing on autonomous vehicles designed specifically for ride-sharing services.
insight: Safety regulations adapt to autonomous vehicle technology advancements.

title: SteamOS comes to a regular gaming desktop with Meta PCs' Steamroller
summary: Steamroller is a mid-range gaming desktop targeting high-frame-rate 1080p play.
insight: Blurs line between console and PC gaming experiences for users.

title: Australia raises social media ban penalties to $99 million as children keep accessing platforms
summary: Australia raises social media ban penalties to $99 million to protect children's online safety.
insight: Tech companies failing to protect children from online harm.

title: TechSpot PC Buying Guide: Four Smart Builds for a Difficult PC Market
summary: This PC buying guide offers four balanced systems for a challenging market. The builds prioritize performance, value, and upgrade paths. Each system is designed to be efficient and cost-effective.
insight: Budget-friendly PC builds require creative component selections now.

title: Netflix now wants every profile to have its own email address, annoying users
summary: Netflix is changing its user identity policy, prompting login prompts and extra steps.
insight: Compromising user convenience for enhanced data collection and security measures.

title: Sony is deleting 551 movies and TV shows you bought on PlayStation, because you don't really own your digital purchases
summary: Sony will remove 551 movies and TV shows from PlayStation Store on September 1, 2026, and delete them from customers' libraries without offering refunds.
insight: Lack of actual ownership of digital purchases is a major concern.

title: Riot's always-on anti-cheat is finally getting an off switch
summary: Riot Games is updating its Vanguard anti-cheat system to no longer run automatically on PC boot, giving players more control over when it's active.
insight: User autonomy and trust are being restored with anti-cheat flexibility.

title: Big Tech's $8 trillion AI bet is making consoles, cars, and electricity more expensive for everyone else
summary: Big Tech's AI development is driving an industrial expansion of data centers, driving up costs for consumers across various industries, including consoles, cars, and electricity.
insight: Massive tech investments boost costs, affecting consumers worldwide.
